Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Diverts flux from geissoschizine toward strychnos alkaloid pathway
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Converts geissoschizine to dehydropreakuammicine, enabling subsequent steps toward strychnine biosynthesis
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate infiltration, LC-MS detection of product
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: BLAST search using CrGO as query, co-expression analysis
Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Metabolic engineering for heterologous production of strychnos alkaloids
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvGO is a cytochrome P450 (CYP71AY6) that oxidizes geissoschizine to dehydropreakuammicine. 2. Expression in N. benthamiana with geissoschizine infiltration yields akuammicine (deformylation product). 3. Essential early step in strychnine biosynthetic pathway.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Ortholog of Catharanthus roseus CrGO; 46% amino acid identity to CrGO

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Hydrolyzes ester moiety of dehydropreakuammicine, triggering decarboxylation to form norfluorocurarine
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Converts dehydropreakuammicine to norfluorocurarine, essential intermediate toward Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with SnvGO and geissoschizine, LC-MS detection of norfluorocurarine
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression analysis with SnvGO as bait
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvNS1 is an α/β hydrolase that hydrolyzes dehydropreakuammicine ester, leading to decarboxylation to norfluorocurarine. 2. Co-expression with SnvGO in N. benthamiana yields norfluorocurarine. 3. Shares 74% identity with SnvNS2 and shows same reactivity.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Two paralogs (SnvNS1 and SnvNS2) identified; SnvNS1 used in subsequent experiments

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Hydroxylates C18 position of norfluorocurarine, enabling subsequent reduction and cyclization
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Converts norfluorocurarine to 18-OH norfluorocurarine, required for Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde formation
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression with SnvGO, SnvNS1, and geissoschizine, LC-MS detection of 18-OH norfluorocurarine
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression analysis with SnvGO as bait
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvNO is a cytochrome P450 (CYP71A144) that hydroxylates norfluorocurarine at C18 to form 18-OH norfluorocurarine. 2. Essential step before reduction by SnvWS. 3. Cannot hydroxylate desoxy Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde, indicating reaction order is oxidation first.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Order of reactions is first oxidation (SnvNO) then reduction (SnvWS)

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Reduces 2,16 double bond and enables spontaneous cyclization to form hemiacetal Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Converts 18-OH norfluorocurarine to Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde, key branchpoint intermediate for strychnine/diaboline
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression with SnvGO, SnvNS1, SnvNO, and geissoschizine, LC-MS detection of Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression analysis with SnvGO as bait
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvWS is an MDR that reduces 2,16 double bond of 18-OH norfluorocurarine (preferred substrate) to form Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde. 2. Catalytic efficiency higher with 18-OH norfluorocurarine (kcat/Km = 0.297 min⁻¹ μM⁻¹) than norfluorocurarine (0.068 min⁻¹ μM⁻¹). 3. Reduction enables spontaneous cyclization to hemiacetal.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Thr95 and Ser309 may hydrogen bond with C18-OH, explaining substrate preference

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Malonylates indole amine of Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde, committing to strychnine branch rather than diaboline (acetylation)
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Catalyzes malonylation to form prestrychnine, which slowly converts non-enzymatically to strychnine/isostrychnine
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in roots, co-expressed with upstream genes (r ≥ 0.99)
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with AAE13 and disodium malonate
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression with upstream genes and AAE13, LC-MS detection of prestrychnine
Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Metabolic engineering for heterologous production; key determinant of strychnine vs diaboline accumulation
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvAT is a BAHD acyltransferase with malonyltransferase activity (vs SpAT acetyltransferase). 2. Malonylates Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde to form prestrychnine (N-malonyl Wieland-Gumlich aldehyde). 3. Prestrychnine slowly converts non-enzymatically to strychnine/isostrychnine. 4. Arg424 residue controls malonyl-CoA selectivity.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: 85% amino acid identity to SpAT but distinct catalytic activity; requires AAE13 for malonyl-CoA supply in heterologous system

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Hydroxylates C10 position of strychnine, first step toward brucine biosynthesis
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Converts strychnine to 10-OH strychnine, enabling subsequent methylation to β-colubrine
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with strychnine infiltration
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression with strychnine substrate, LC-MS detection of 10-OH strychnine
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Co-expression analysis with SnvGO as bait
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Snv10H is a cytochrome P450 (CYP82D367) that hydroxylates strychnine at C10 to form 10-OH strychnine. 2. First hydroxylation step in brucine biosynthesis pathway. 3. Expression in N. benthamiana with strychnine yields 10-OH strychnine.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: One of 12 cytochrome P450s tested that showed this activity

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Methylates 10-OH strychnine to β-colubrine, and also methylates 11-OH strychnine to α-colubrine, 10-deMe brucine to brucine
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Catalyzes O-methylation steps in brucine biosynthesis, converting hydroxylated intermediates to methoxy derivatives
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana, in vitro assays with purified protein
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression with Snv10H and strychnine, LC-MS detection of β-colubrine; in vitro methylation assays
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selection of methyltransferases highly expressed in roots
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvOMT is an O-methyltransferase that methylates 10-OH strychnine to β-colubrine. 2. Also methylates 11-OH strychnine to α-colubrine and 10-deMe brucine to brucine. 3. Essential for installing methoxy groups in brucine biosynthesis.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Shows broader substrate specificity, methylating multiple hydroxylated strychnine derivatives

Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Hydroxylates C11 position of β-colubrine, enabling final methylation to brucine
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: Converts β-colubrine to 11-deMe brucine, final hydroxylation step in brucine biosynthesis
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with β-colubrine or strychnine/Snv10H/SnvOMT
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression with β-colubrine substrate, LC-MS detection of 11-deMe brucine; combinatorial expression yields brucine
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Targeted cytochrome P450s within CYP71 clade strongly expressed in roots
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Snv11H is a cytochrome P450 (CYP71AH44) that hydroxylates β-colubrine at C11 to form 11-deMe brucine. 2. Expression with β-colubrine alone yields 11-deMe brucine; with SnvOMT yields brucine. 3. Final hydroxylation step in brucine pathway.
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: One of 3 cytochrome P450s within CYP71 clade tested; does not hydroxylate β-colubrine without SnvOMT present for complete conversion to brucine
